Israel is here called Jeshurun, which means "the upright one." Only those are truly Israelites who have no deceit in them. God owns those who serve him. He will help them overcome difficulties and in their services. Water is a symbol of the Holy Spirit; just as water refreshes, cleanses, and makes the earth fruitful, so do his influences affect the soul. This gift of the Holy Spirit is the great blessing, the plentiful outpouring of which God kept for the latter days. Wherever God gives his Spirit, he will give all other blessings. By this, there shall be a great increase of the church; it will be spread to distant places. Was there any other Rock or Protector that could defend them? None besides could foretell these things to come, of which God by his prophets gave notice. All was set in order in the Divine predictions as well as in the Divine purposes. Could anyone else have done so? Who can compare with Israel's Redeemer and King?

Image-making is described to expose the folly of idolaters. Though a man had used part of a log for fuel, he fell down before an image made of the remainder, praying it to deliver him. Man greatly dishonors God when he represents him after the image of man. Satan blinds the eyes of unbelievers, causing absurd reasonings in matters of religion. Whether men seek happiness in worldly things or run into unbelief, superstition, or any false system, they feed on ashes. A heart deceived by pride, love of sin, and departure from God turns people away from his holy truth and worship. While affections are corrupted, a person holds fast to lies as their best treasure. Are our hearts set upon the wealth of the world and its pleasures? They will certainly prove to be lies. If we trust in outward professions and doings, as if those would save us, we deceive ourselves. Self-suspicion is the first step toward self-deliverance. Whoever wants to deliver his soul must question his conscience: Is there not a lie in my right hand?

Return to me. It is the great concern of those who have backslidden from God, like the Jews of old, to hasten their return to him. The work of redemption accomplished for us by Christ encourages hope for all blessings from him. Our transgressions and our sins are as a thick cloud between heaven and earth: sins separate us from God; they threaten a storm of wrath. When God pardons sin, he blots out, he dispels this cloud, this thick cloud, so that the way to heaven is open again. The cloud is scattered by the Sun of righteousness; it is completely gone. The comforts that flow into the soul when sin is forgiven are like clear shining after clouds and rain. Let not Israel be discouraged; nothing is too hard for God: having made all, he can make whatever use he pleases of anything. Those who learn to know Christ see all knowledge as foolishness in comparison with the knowledge of him. And his enemies will find their counsels turned into foolishness, and themselves caught in their craftiness. The exact fulfillment of the prophecies of Scripture confirms the truth of the whole and proves its Divine origin. The particular favors God designed for his people in captivity were foretold here long before they went into captivity. Very great difficulties would be in the way of their deliverance; but it is promised that by Divine power they all should be removed. God knew who should be the Deliverer of his people; and let his church know it, so that when they heard such a name talked about, they might know their redemption was drawing near. It is the greatest honor of the greatest men to be employed as instruments of the Divine favor to his people. In things wherein people serve themselves and look no further, God makes them do all his pleasure. And a nobler shepherd than Cyrus does his Father’s will until his work is fully completed.
